summary: Iteration 259: Backend testing of email architecture changes. All 28 tests passed (100%). Key verifications: partner signup uses correct trigger=partner_verification (not old verification), Zoho Mail delivers it with status=sent, platform admin sees all restricted templates, partner admin is filtered, ensure_seeded prunes platform-only templates from partner tenants, 2-tier provider lookup is correct, Zoho access_token caching is working.

minor.issue: Partner tenant (test-hardening-fix4) had 16 templates in DB including 4 partner_billing templates before ensure_seeded was triggered. After first API call, ensure_seeded pruned them correctly. This means any NEW partner tenants created before the pruning code was added may still have stale partner_billing templates until an admin first hits the templates API.
minor.priority: LOW — self-heals on first API call

critical_code_review_comments: 2-tier logic in email_service.py: effective_tid = tenant_id if tenant_id else 'automate-accounts' — this means customer signup emails (tenant_id=partner_tenant, no email provider) will be MOCKED, not sent via platform Zoho. This is correct per the new spec but is a behavioral change from iter258 which showed platform fallback for partner tenant emails. Context: iter258's customer signup tests were testing OLD behavior (platform fallback for any tenant with no provider). The NEW spec restricts fallback to ONLY when tenant_id=None.

ensure_seeded is called on EVERY email send AND on every list_templates request. For tenants with many templates this could be a minor performance concern, but the existing_count check makes it fast for already-seeded tenants.

Zoho access_token caching is now working correctly (added in this iteration). Token is cached for ~50 minutes in oauth_connections.credentials. This fixes the iter258 critical bug of token exhaustion.

verified_scenarios.partner_signup_trigger_partner_verificat: PASS — email_logs shows trigger=partner_verification (not old verification trigger)
verified_scenarios.partner_signup_subject_with_store_name: PASS — subject='Verify your Automate Accounts partner account'
verified_scenarios.platform_admin_sees_partner_verification: PASS — GET /api/admin/email-templates returns partner_verification with category=platform_admin_only
verified_scenarios.platform_admin_sees_partner_billing: PASS — 4 partner_billing templates visible to platform admin
verified_scenarios.partner_admin_no_platform_admin_only: PASS — partner admin sees 0 platform_admin_only templates
verified_scenarios.partner_admin_no_partner_billing: PASS — partner admin sees 0 partner_billing templates
verified_scenarios.ensure_seeded_prunes_partner_billing_fro: PASS — DB verified: 0 partner_billing templates in partner tenant after ensure_seeded
verified_scenarios.ensure_seeded_prunes_platform_admin_only: PASS — DB verified: 0 platform_admin_only templates in partner tenant
verified_scenarios.platform_zoho_connection_validated: PASS — automate-accounts has is_validated=True Zoho Mail connection
verified_scenarios.zoho_access_token_caching: PASS — access_token and access_token_expires_at cached in DB credentials
verified_scenarios.two_tier_provider_lookup_no_global_tier: PASS — No email connections with tenant_id=None/empty exist in DB
verified_scenarios.partner_signup_fallback_to_platform: PASS — tenant_id=None routes to platform (automate-accounts) connection

test_credentials: Platform Super Admin: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123! | Partner Admin: test_admin_fix4@example.com / TestAdmin123! (partner_code=test-hardening-fix4)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All email architecture changes verified. The partner signup now uses trigger=partner_verification and sends via Zoho Mail. Zoho access_token caching is working (fixes iter258 token exhaustion bug). Template visibility filtering is correct (platform admin sees all, partner admin sees only non-restricted). ensure_seeded pruning removes partner_billing/platform_admin_only templates from partner tenants. Note: customer signup emails with tenant_id=partner_tenant (no email provider) are MOCKED per new 2-tier spec — no platform fallback when tenant_id is set.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Previous email architecture had 3-tier lookup (tenant → global → mocked). New 2-tier spec removes global tier: tenant_id present → tenant's connection only; tenant_id=None → platform (automate-accounts) connection.
fixes_verified: 1. trigger=partner_verification added to register-partner endpoint (was verification)
2. partner_verification template added with category=platform_admin_only in _TEMPLATES
3. list_templates filters by role: platform admin sees all, partner admin filters out platform_admin_only + partner_billing
4. ensure_seeded prunes platform_admin_only + partner_billing from non-platform tenants
5. 2-tier provider lookup: effective_tid = tenant_id if tenant_id else 'automate-accounts'
6. Zoho Mail from_email validation in oauth.py: checks from_email against Zoho account emailAddress list
